name: gitbash-clipboard-cd
description: Windows Git Bash helpers (cdc / cdh) to cd into folder paths copied from Explorer — fixes backslash-path mangling and reads clipboard history.

methods.name: copy-and-source
methods.command:
cp $HOME/.claude/.skill-repos/claude-skills/skill-groups/gitbash-clipboard-cd/files/cdh-cliphist.ps1 $HOME/.cdh-cliphist.ps1 && cp $HOME/.claude/.skill-repos/claude-skills/skill-groups/gitbash-clipboard-cd/files/clipboard-cd.sh $HOME/.clipboard-cd.sh && { grep -q clipboard-cd.sh $HOME/.bashrc 2>/dev/null || printf '
# gitbash-clipboard-cd skill (cdc / cdh)
[ -f ~/.clipboard-cd.sh ] && source ~/.clipboard-cd.sh
' >> $HOME/.bashrc; }

install.notes: Windows-only. Copies the cdh PowerShell helper to ~/.cdh-cliphist.ps1 and the shell functions to ~/.clipboard-cd.sh, then adds a source line to ~/.bashrc (idempotent). The install check uses cmp so a git pull that changes either file triggers a re-copy on the next install. Avoid double quotes in check/command — the installer's field extractor truncates at the first quote.

post_install_hints: Open a new Git Bash window (or run: source ~/.bashrc) to load cdc / cdh.
cdh requires Windows Clipboard History — enable it at Settings -> System -> Clipboard, or press Win+V and turn it on.
Usage: copy a folder in Explorer (or right-click -> Copy as path), then type cdc (current clipboard) or cdh (most recent directory in clipboard history).
